title: Emplectocladus glandulosus
intro: Emplectocladus glandulosus, also known as glandular helecho, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of E. glandulosus include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, E. glandulosus is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Emplectocladus glandulosus is a plant species.
